_README: Shared, cross-cutting watch sources referenced by id from per-library watch.json
(source_ids[]). Per-expert-only sources live in that library's watch.json extra_sources[]
instead. The deterministic discover step (watch_lib.py) fetches feed_url, validates it
(status + content-type + min length + no interstitial marker), and on any failure records
the source 'unreachable' - it NEVER fabricates items.
IMPORTANT: feed URLs below are CANDIDATES marked "verified": false. The first real
discovery run confirms each one resolves to a parseable feed/sitemap; if it does not, it
is logged unreachable and you fix feed_url here. Do not assume an unverified feed is live.
Seeded 2026-06-27 from the sources you approved (Anthropic sections, Claude Code docs,
cadence: 'fast' sources are crawled by Claude_PracticeWatch_Fast (twice weekly) AND the
weekly all-run; 'weekly' sources only by the all-run.

sources.name: Anthropic Engineering blog
sources.fetch_notes: Index page is likely JS-rendered; prefer a sitemap/RSS if one resolves. Filter sitemap to /engineering/ paths. If only the JS shell returns, mark unreachable and revisit feed_url.

sources.name: Anthropic News
sources.fetch_notes: Filter sitemap to /news/ paths. Model launches land here - high relevance to claude / claude-code experts (new pages only for docs-mirror libs).

sources.name: Anthropic Research / Alignment
sources.fetch_notes: Filter sitemap to /research/ paths. Feeds karpathy/agent-eval angle, not the docs-mirror libs.

sources.name: Claude Code docs changelog / new pages
sources.fetch_notes: For docs-mirror libs (claude-code) this surfaces NEW pages/changelog entries only - content currency stays on refresh_libraries.ps1. Confirm the changelog/sitemap URL on first run.

sources.name: Kaggle blog + competition writeups
sources.fetch_notes: Kaggle's own pages are a JS app shell - use the blog RSS (Medium-hosted) or its API. A winning-solution writeup hosted on Medium is Tier 0 by association but confirm authorship; generic Medium stays Tier 3 (lead-only).

sources.name: Simon Willison's Weblog
sources.fetch_notes: High-volume practitioner; prolific on LLM tooling and agentic workflows. Confirm the atom URL on first run.

sources.name: Eugene Yan
sources.fetch_notes: Applied-ML and LLM-eval practitioner; relevant to karpathy + agent-eval angles.

sources.name: Lil'Log (Lilian Weng)
sources.fetch_notes: Infrequent but high-signal deep dives (agents, hallucination, RL).

sources.name: Hamel Husain
sources.fetch_notes: Strong on LLM evals and practical applied-ML workflow.

sources.name: Andrej Karpathy (blog)
sources.fetch_notes: Karpathy posts rarely but it's canonical for karpathy-expert. X is Tier-3 lead-only (x.com blocks direct fetch); use a readable mirror, never the live timeline.

sources.name: Addy Osmani
sources.fetch_notes: Coined 'loop engineering'; relevant to boris/agentic-coding practice. Feed verified live 2026-06-27: blog/rss/ and /rss both 404; /feed.xml and /rss.xml return valid RSS (newest item 2026-06-16).
